sanskrit:
नादत्ते कस्यचित्पापं न चैव सुकृतं विभुः।
अज्ञानेनावृतं ज्ञानं तेन मुह्यन्ति जन्तवः॥
transliteration:
nādatte kasyacit pāpaṃ na caiva sukṛtaṃ vibhuḥ |
ajñānenāvṛtaṃ jñānaṃ tena muhyanti jantavaḥ ||
literal_meaning: The Supreme (vibhuḥ) does not take upon anyone’s sin (pāpam) nor even merit (sukṛtam); knowledge (jñānam) is covered (āvṛtam) by ignorance (ajñānena); therefore (tena) beings (jantavaḥ) are deluded (muhyanti).
contextual_meaning: The psyche’s distress is not divinely inflicted; it is a self-generated trance produced by the veiling of innate wisdom. Once ignorance is removed, the person sees that responsibility—and therefore agency—always rested within.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance dissolving into empowered self-ownership once the veil of ignorance is recognized.
trigger_scenario: After a breakup you say, 'The universe is punishing me,' until therapy reveals how your own unrecognized patterns created the pain; the insight frees you to choose differently.
anchor_text: Your confusion lifts once you reclaim the wisdom already inside you.
